source_text: the model gives itself more context before it answers the question
the system allows itself one more revision before it emits the answer
the policy gives itself a wider margin before it commits the next word
the decoder gives itself more evidence before it finalizes the phrase
the runtime gives itself a short pause before it accepts the risky token
the translator gives itself more room before it rewrites the ending
the observer keeps itself active while the engine continues the draft
the aligner gives itself a little more signal before it trusts the prefix
the method gives itself one more word before it commits the translation
the model keeps itself conservative while it follows the live source
we keep only a simple and exact reconstruction of the draft to source block
we build only a compact and faithful replay of the relevant attention rows
the method uses a small and stable set of translation alignment heads
the policy relies on a strict and monotone source side frontier
the observer returns a compact and engine native capture of query and key tensors
the paper reports a clear and reproducible comparison of the three capture seams
the runtime exposes a single and role agnostic AlignAtt substrate
the benchmark isolates a clean and measurable overhead from attention capture
the translation uses a natural and visibly reordered French phrasing
the figure highlights a sparse and multilingual family of alignment heads
we carefully keep only the attention rows that the policy actually consumes
we deliberately retain only the prompt keys that the replay really needs
we selectively reconstruct only the source slice that drives the acceptance rule
we explicitly compare only the seams that expose a real capture path
we now measure directly how much faster the engine native observer runs
we intentionally show only the words that remain safe under the frontier
we consistently preserve only the accepted prefix while the draft keeps growing
we separately benchmark the eager path before we compare it with qk fast
we visually separate the prompt from the source before we inspect the draft
we intentionally place the accepted prefix before we continue the translation
